title: Major escalation in Iran war as Tehran targets energy facilities in Qatar and Saudi Arabia
description: Iran has targeted energy facilities in Qatar and Saudi Arabia – a major escalation in the conflict which could threaten the global supply of oil and gas and drive up prices.  An oil refinery in Saudi Arabia was damaged by debris from drones, which were intercepted but fell onto the facility.  Meanwhile, Qatar had to shut down the world’s largest natural gas plant after it was hit by Iranian missiles.

title: ‘The next phase will be even more punishing on Iran’: Trump admin’s dire threat to Tehran
description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has warned that the “hardest hits are yet to come” for Iran.  “I’m not going to give away the details of our tactical efforts, but the hardest hits are yet to come from the US military,” he said.  “The next phase will be even more punishing on Iran than it is right now.”

title: ‘It cannot be trusted’: Angus Taylor blasts Albanese government over failed promises
description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has stated the Albanese government “cannot be trusted” as inflation and high interest rates have “beaten” Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and Treasurer Jim Chalmers.

title: Massive explosion seen in Beirut amid Israel’s continued strikes on Lebanon
description: Footage has captured the moment Beirut was recently hit amid Israel’s continued attacks on Lebanon after Hezbollah fired rockets and drones at Haifa.

title: Iranians do the 'Trump dance' to YMCA to thank the president for killing Ayatollah Khamenei
description: Iranians from across the world have thanked US President Donald Trump by doing his iconic dance following the lethal strikes on Iran.   On Saturday, the US and Israel struck Iran, leading to the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.   Following the strikes and announcement of Khamenei’s death, Iranians were captured singing and dancing to YMCA, mimicking Trump’s iconic dance.   Celebrations were even seen outside the Israeli embassy in Berlin, Germany, with large crowds celebrating to YMCA.

title: US strike on Iran complied with the law: Rubio
description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the strike on Iran fully complied with the law and Congress was briefed in advance.  “We notified Congress, we notified the Gang of Eight, we notified congressional leadership. No law requires us to do that,” Mr Rubio said.  “We did notify members of Congress in advance … we’ve complied with the law 100 per cent.”

title: Marco Rubio says the ‘hardest hit’ is yet to come in dire warning to Iran
description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has said the “hardest hit” on Iran is yet to come while taking questions from the press on Capitol Hill.  Rubio’s comments come during a joint US-Israeli major combat on Iran in which Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in a missile strike.  Iran has aggressively retaliated, striking several US-allied countries across the Middle East, forcing the airspace above and the Strait of Hormuz to close.  The secretary of state goes on to say the “next phase” will be even more punishing for Iran, vowing the operation will continue until the US “objectives” are achieved.  Rubio also spoke on how US President Donald Trump would love to see the Iranian people rise up and overthrow the regime.

title: Bill Clinton shockingly clears Donald Trump of any wrongdoing with Jeffrey Epstein
description: Former US President Bill Clinton has asserted that Donald Trump is clear of any wrongdoing in relation to paedophile Jeffrey Epstein.  “Since there was no follow-up question. He never, the president [Trump] never… this is 20-something years ago, never said anything to me to make me think he was involved in anything improper in regard to Epstein. He just didn’t,” Clinton said.  Clinton’s comments came during his behind-closed-door deposition with the House Oversight Committee on his connection to Epstein.  During the hearing, Clinton was asked about his relationship with the paedophile and the infamous photo of himself in a hot tub with an unknown female.  Clinton responded “no” when asked if he had any sexual relations with the lady in the hot tub, with another image showing the 79-year-old with Ghislaine Maxwell.

title: ‘Enemy of the West’: Iran faces ‘fully justified’ onslaught by US-Israel strikes
description: Israel’s Ambassador to Australia Dr Hillel Newman claims the US-Israel strikes on Iran are fully justified.  “Anyone who critiques the attack on Iran, I think, has lost their moral compass completely,” Mr Newman told Sky News Australia.  “There is full justification for this attack.  “Iran, since the Islamic Revolution in 1979, has turned itself into the enemy of the West.”

title: Hezbollah and Israel continue to trade strikes
description: Hezbollah has launched another attack from Beirut at Israel.  This comes as Israel has continued its attacks on southern Lebanon after Hezbollah fired rockets and drones at Haifa.

title: US Speaker Mike Johnson insists Iran regime change wasn’t the objective
description: US House Speaker Mike Johnson says the objective of Operation Epic Fury was to take out Iran’s missiles, not regime change.  “Iran was a great threat to everybody in the region and everybody in the world. It was an evil regime,” Mr Johnson said.  “That was not the mission of the US to go and take out the regime, but that happened, and in my estimation, that is a great development for freedom-loving people around the world.”

title: US warns inaction on Iran could have been ‘devastating’
description: US House Speaker Mike Johnson says the US‑Israeli operation against Iran was a “defensive measure” aimed at protecting the American people from Iranian threats.  “Israel was determined to act in their own defence, with or without American support,” Mr Johnson said.  “If Israel fired upon Iran … they would of imediately retaliated against US personnel and assets.  “The consequences of inaction on our part could have been devastating.”

title: Fire and minor damage at US embassy in Riyadh after being hit by drones
description: The US embassy in Saudi Arabia’s capital Riyadh has been attacked by two drones, resulting in a limited fire and minor damage.

title: Victorian government pushes ahead with work from home laws with no small business exemptions
description: The Victorian government is pushing ahead with its work from home laws, announcing there will be no exemptions for small businesses.  The draft laws are expected to be introduced to parliament and passed by the end of the year.  Under the proposal, employees would be entitled to work from home at least two days a week.

title: Gulf states predicted to ‘join forces’ with Israel and the US
description: Former White House chief of staff Mick Mulvaney weighs in on the potential for the Gulf states to become involved in the escalating Iran crisis.  “I don’t think you’ll see the Gulf states pressuring the US to dial it back,” Mr Mulvaney told Sky News Australia.  “I think you’ll see the Gulf states joining in with the US and Israel to sort this as quickly as possible.  “Face it, they have no love lost for Iran anyway … I think the dynamic is in America’s favour as far as the Gulf states go.”
